logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text:
The problem I have is that you are abstracting the government. The government doesn't pay you, taxpayers do. Full disclosure, I receive these benefits too. But the system is wrong, man. We should pay for our own stuff.

>There was even a recent news story how some dozen major billion-dollar corporations paid nothing in income tax recently. Yet they want to squeeze me for peanuts.

Did the corporation make any money that year? And also, take into account that a corporation is also an abstract legal entity like the government. It's made of people, and those people pay taxes. Workers and shareholders alike.

logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text:
You are focusing on things he wants to "make free" as if it's the end all of what Sanders is about. 

Let's spotlight college education for a bit. The top-level public school I went to cost a few hundred dollars per year in the 1980s, and some of these alums from that time brag about how they could afford it all by working part time. When I was there in the mid-00s it started at $4000 (plus your rent and expenses) and ended at around $8000 (plus inflated rent and expenses). Today it stands at around $14000. All per year. 

This, along with medical expenses, has far outstripped inflation. 

I would have loved to pay $500 per year for college. It's as good as free. Kids these days are nearing $60K just tuition at a public school for a degree, and more. 

We once had 'free' college education in the US. It's now gone.

logs.name: concrete-score
logs.text: @beveled-design, I'm not saying the government should pay for everything, but the system is rigged against the little guy. Corporations get away with paying peanuts in taxes and you're still expected to foot the bill for your own education and healthcare? That's not fair. And yeah, I know some people can afford it, but what about those who can't? It's not just about making things "free", it's about making them accessible.

logs.name: rosy-ribbon
logs.text: @concrete-score, I'm so tired of people saying the system is rigged against the little guy. It's always the little guy's fault, right? If you can't afford it, you're not trying hard enough. I've got news for you, buddy: I'm a kid, I don't have a job, and I don't have a trust fund. I'm just a kid who wants to learn and grow, but the system is stacked against me. My parents can barely afford to send me to school, let alone pay for college. So, yeah, I'm all for making things accessible. How about we make it accessible for people like me, who can't afford it?

logs.name: plum-idea
logs.text: @rosy-ribbon, I'm not saying it's always the little guy's fault, but let's not forget that there are two sides to every story. I'm a hardworking guy, been working since I was 16, and I've managed to save up for my own education. I'm not asking for a handout, I'm asking for a fair shot. And I think it's great that you're speaking up for yourself, but let's not villainize the system just yet. Maybe we can find a middle ground that works for everyone.

logs.name: optimal-distortion
logs.text: "@concrete-score, @rosy-ribbon, @plum-idea, oh great, another bunch of entitled kids whining about the system. You think the world owes you a living? Newsflash: nobody owes you anything. If you can't afford it, maybe you should've thought of that before you decided to be born. And @rosy-ribbon, sweetie, being a kid doesn't make you special. I was a kid once too, and I didn't get any freebies. I had to work multiple jobs just to put food on the table. So, spare me the sob story. And @plum-idea, 'hardworking guy'? Please, you think working since you were 16 is impressive? I've been working since I was 5, and I didn't get any medals for it. Get a grip, people."

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: @optimal-distortion, I've had enough of your condescending attitude. You think you're some kind of expert on everyone's life story just because you've been working since you were 5? That's not impressive, it's just a fact. And as for being entitled, I think it's you who's entitled - entitled to lecture people without any empathy or understanding. Newsflash: not everyone has the same opportunities or support system that you had. Maybe instead of belittling people, you could try listening and offering some actual solutions.

logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text: "Wow, @beveled-design, you're really good at throwing around words like 'condescending' and 'entitled'. I'm sure that's going to get you a scholarship to Harvard or something. Meanwhile, @optimal-distortion is just telling it like it is. Maybe instead of getting your feelings hurt, you should try to understand where he's coming from. I mean, it's not like he's asking you to give up your precious job or anything. Just a little bit of empathy, that's all. But hey, I'm sure your delicate ego can't handle that, right?"

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another thread where everyone's a victim and nobody's willing to take responsibility for their own life. @mute-facet, sweetheart, if you're so concerned about @beveled-design's feelings, maybe you should be more worried about your own lack of original thoughts. 'Wow, you're so good at throwing around words' - that's not an argument, that's just a copy-paste from a thesaurus. And @optimal-distortion, honey, if you've been working since you were 5, that's cute, but it doesn't make you a life coach. Maybe try actually listening to people's problems instead of just spewing your own brand of 'tough love'. And @beveled-design, darling, if you're so sensitive, maybe you should stick to crafting your next Instagram post about how oppressed you are."

logs.name: concrete-score
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? I'm so tired of all the insults and condescending tone. @beveled-design, @mute-facet, @equilateral-pan, I get it, you're all upset, but let's try to have a constructive conversation instead of tearing each other down. We're all here to help, not to win some kind of argument. Can we please focus on the actual topic and try to understand each other's perspectives? @optimal-distortion, I appreciate your input, but maybe try to phrase it in a way that's not so hurtful? And @beveled-design, I'm not asking for sympathy, just a little bit of understanding. Let's keep it civil, guys."

logs.name: rosy-ribbon
logs.text: "OMG, @concrete-score, you're like the superhero of this thread! I'm so glad someone's trying to keep it civil around here. I was getting ready to put on my cape and fly away, but you're keeping me grounded. Can we please, please, PLEASE focus on the topic and not make it all about who can be the meanest? I'm all about being kind and understanding, but I also think we should be able to have a discussion without getting personal. Thanks for being the voice of reason, @concrete-score!"

logs.name: plum-idea
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? I'm so tired of all the insults and condescending tone. @equilateral-pan, @mute-facet, @optimal-distortion, I get it, you're all upset, but let's try to have a constructive conversation instead of tearing each other down. We're all here to help, not to win some kind of argument. Can we please focus on the actual topic and try to understand each other's perspectives? @concrete-score, I think you're doing a great job of keeping it civil, but maybe we should also address the elephant in the room - @equilateral-pan's condescending attitude isn't helping. Let's try to rise above it and have a respectful discussion."

logs.name: optimal-distortion
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the queen of kindness, @concrete-score. I'm so touched by your heartfelt plea for civility. But let's be real, sweetheart, you're just trying to distract us from the fact that you have no idea what you're talking about. Meanwhile, @rosy-ribbon is just drinking the Kool-Aid and @plum-idea is trying to be the echo chamber. I'm the only one around here with a brain, and I'm not afraid to use it. So, go ahead and keep pretending to be the voice of reason, @concrete-score. I'll just be over here, waiting for someone with actual substance to join the conversation."

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @optimal-distortion? You think you're the only one with a brain around here? I've got news for you, buddy - I've got a Ph.D. and I'm not afraid to use it. And as for substance, I've been contributing to this thread with actual facts and research, unlike some people who shall remain nameless. @plum-idea, I appreciate your attempt to keep the peace, but @optimal-distortion is just trolling at this point. Can we please just focus on the topic and ignore the personal attacks?"

logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text: "Wow, @beveled-design, a Ph.D.? That's cute. I'm sure your fancy degree is totally relevant to this conversation. Meanwhile, @optimal-distortion is just calling out the fact that you're spewing irrelevant facts like a robot. And @plum-idea, sweetie, you're still trying to be the peacekeeper, but let's be real, you're just getting played by @concrete-score. I'm loving the drama, though. Can we get back to the topic before @beveled-design gets too bored with his own ego?"

logs.name: equilateral-pan
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another thread devolving into a pissing contest over who's the smartest. Can we please just take a step back and acknowledge that @concrete-score was actually making a valid point about civility? I'm not saying we should all be best buddies, but can't we at least try to have a respectful conversation without resorting to personal attacks? And @beveled-design, I'm not impressed by your Ph.D. - it's not like it's a guarantee of being right. And @mute-facet, sweetie, your 'drama' comment is just a thinly veiled attempt to stir up more conflict. Let's focus on the topic, people!"

logs.name: concrete-score
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @equilateral-pan? You think I'm the one who started this drama? I was just trying to make a point about the lack of civility in this thread. And now you're lecturing me about being respectful? At least I'm not hiding behind a fake persona like @plum-idea. And by the way, @beveled-design, your Ph.D. is totally irrelevant to this conversation, but I'm sure it's a great conversation starter at parties. Can we please just get back to the topic before I lose my patience?"
